title: Detecting Palindromic Triples in an Array

statement.markdown: Given an array of integers, write a function to detect whether there exists a palindromic triple within the array. A palindromic triple is a sequence of three distinct numbers that form a palindrome when concatenated. For example, given the array `[1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6]`, the triple `[1, 2, 2]` is not a palindrome since it is not a sequence of distinct numbers. However, the triple `[1, 2, 1]` is not a palindrome since it's not a palindrome when concatenated. However, the triple `[1, 2, 3]` is not a palindrome since `[1, 2, 3]` is not the same forwards and backwards. However, the triple `[1, 2, 3]` is not a palindrome. The triple `[2, 2, 2]` is a palindrome since it is the same forwards and backwards. However, the triple `[2, 3, 3]` is not a palindrome since it is not the same forwards and backwards. However, the triple `[3, 4, 4]` is not a palindrome since it is not the same forwards and backwards. However, the triple `[1, 2, 3]` is not a palindrome since it is not the same forwards and backwards. However, the triple `[1, 2, 2]` is not a palindrome since it is not a sequence of distinct numbers. However, the triple `[1, 2, 1]` is not a palindrome since it is not the same forwards and backwards. However, the triple `[1, 2, 3]` is not a palindrome since it is not the same forwards and backwards. However, the triple `[1, 2, 3]` is not a palindrome since it is not the same forwards and backwards.
statement.inputFormat: An array of integers, for example: `[1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6]`
statement.outputFormat: A boolean indicating whether a palindromic triple exists in the array
statement.notes: Note that a palindromic triple is a sequence of three distinct numbers that form a palindrome when concatenated. For example, the array `[1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6]` contains the palindromic triple `[2, 3, 2]`.

constraints.description: The length of the input array

examples.explanation: The triple `[2, 3, 2]` is a palindrome since it is the same forwards and backwards.
There is no palindromic triple in this array.
The triple `[2, 2, 2]` is a palindrome since it is the same forwards and backwards.

hints.text: Consider using a set to keep track of the distinct elements in the array.

editorial.title: Detecting Palindromic Triples in an Array
editorial.intuition: The problem can be solved by iterating over the array and checking for palindromic triples.
editorial.bruteForce: The brute force approach would be to generate all possible triplets and check if they are palindromes.
editorial.optimalSolution: The optimal solution involves iterating over the array and checking for palindromic triples.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The correctness of the optimal solution can be proven by showing that it covers all possible cases.
editorial.dryRun: Consider using a dry run to test the solution with sample inputs.
commonMistakes: Not considering the order of the triple

buggyStarterCode.cpp: class Solution { public: bool findPalindromicTriple(vector<int>& nums) { for (int i = 0; i < nums.size(); i++) { for (int j = i + 1; j < nums.size(); j++) { for (int k = j + 1; k < nums.size(); k++) { if (nums[i] == nums[j] || nums[j] == nums[k] || nums[k] == nums[i]) { continue; } if (nums[i] == nums[j] && nums[j] == nums[k]) { return true; } } } } return false; } };
buggyStarterCode.java: public class Solution { public boolean findPalindromicTriple(int[] nums) { for (int i = 0; i < nums.length; i++) { for (int j = i + 1; j < nums.length; j++) { for (int k = j + 1; k < nums.length; k++) { if (nums[i] == nums[j] || nums[j] == nums[k] || nums[k] == nums[i]) { continue; } if (nums[i] == nums[j] && nums[j] == nums[k]) { return true; } } } } return false; } }
buggyStarterCode.javascript: function findPalindromicTriple(nums) { for (let i = 0; i < nums.length; i++) { for (let j = i + 1; j < nums.length; j++) { for (let k = j + 1; k < nums.length; k++) { if (nums[i] == nums[j] || nums[j] == nums[k] || nums[k] == nums[i]) { continue; } if (nums[i] == nums[j] && nums[j] == nums[k]) { return true; } } } } return false; }

solutions.java:
// Solution
public class Solution { public boolean findPalindromicTriple(int[] nums) { for (int i = 0; i < nums.length; i++) { for (int j = i + 1; j < nums.length; j++) { for (int k = j + 1; k < nums.length; k++) { if (nums[i] != nums[j] && nums[j] != nums[k] && nums[k] != nums[i]) { String s = Integer.toString(nums[i]) + Integer.toString(nums[j]) + Integer.toString(nums[k]); String r = s; r = new StringBuilder(r).reverse().toString(); if (s.equals(r)) { return true; } } } } } return false; } }
solutions.javascript:
// Solution
function findPalindromicTriple(nums) { for (let i = 0; i < nums.length; i++) { for (let j = i + 1; j < nums.length; j++) { for (let k = j + 1; k < nums.length; k++) { if (nums[i] != nums[j] && nums[j] != nums[k] && nums[k] != nums[i]) { let s = nums[i].toString() + nums[j].toString() + nums[k].toString(); let r = s.split('').reverse().join(''); if (s === r) { return true; } } } } } return false; }
